trans-unit.source: Exercises Module 6 - Data Analysis with QGIS
trans-unit.target: Exercises Module 6 - Data Analysis with QGIS

trans-unit.source: These exercises cover all the content from Module 6.
trans-unit.target: These exercises cover all the content from Module 6.

trans-unit.source: We want to create an overview of different vulnerability indicators. From the Covid-19 risk indicators dataset we take `% permanent wall type`, `% permanent roof type` and `poverty incidence`. From the Uganda population statistics, we calculate the `% of under fives` and `% of elderly`. By combining the data, we are now able to visualise the areas in Uganda that are most vulnerable.
trans-unit.target: We want to create an overview of different vulnerability indicators. From the Covid-19 risk indicators dataset we take `% permanent wall type`, `% permanent roof type` and `poverty incidence`. From the Uganda population statistics, we calculate the `% of under fives` and `% of elderly`. By combining the data, we are now able to visualise the areas in Uganda that are most vulnerable.

trans-unit.source: We want to create an overview of different vulnerability indicators. From the Covid-19 risk indicators dataset we take % permanent wall type, % permanent roof type and poverty incidence. From the Uganda population statistics we calculate the % of under fives and % of elderly. We combine the data and we are now able to visualise the areas in Uganda that are most vulnerable.
trans-unit.target: We want to create an overview of different vulnerability indicators. From the Covid-19 risk indicators dataset we take % permanent wall type, % permanent roof type and poverty incidence. From the Uganda population statistics we calculate the % of under fives and % of elderly. We combine the data and we are now able to visualise the areas in Uganda that are most vulnerable.
